change_comments.message: Maybe we do a quick walkthrough of the comments later. SOme might need a bit of discussion ie they are more like proposal

inline_comments.message:
I suspect this commit is doing much more than that..

would like to see at performance type test to see how this performs...
(separate ticket can be done after delivery)
use classNameToErrorType static map. and the all the exception handler method probably can be combine into one...
extact out common extracTReason to remove duplication

suggestio, instead of springboot exception handling use Plain old Jav to directly build the error response here
still ahv a class called ProvMnsErrorResponseBuilder
i thought youre changing this with a handler class

this method only returns error response if its null if im right, it was a bit unclear from just reading this part and error response is not used anywhere else

either change the method name or refactor for clarity
can this be changed ?if so maybe mention put and delete for this
should be mentioned  in the commit message, this is a very important change!

it also seem bad separation of functionality
ie a class called ....Mapper does validation  either split into different classes or renamed the mapper if it does a lot more then just mapping
introduce local var for better readability
I suppose we didn't define these strings so we cannot calidy this btter?

familiar one: httpStatus, (almost) never shorten instance names
(self review should have spotted this :-) )
NB: a bit OTT , do we really need this method? SUppose depends on hwo often it is called

can relax this when throwing exception ie just use *_

not correct!

incorrect, describe expected status code instead

Unfortunately these 'types' have been defined by the documentation
I could be called from 4 different places in the controller, added it to make it easier to change if needed in future
Cheap solution, let me think of a better name when I review that class

you now have at least 3 classes in rest.util starting with 'ProvMns'
maybe time for a rest.provmns package ?! Then maybe the classes dont have to start with ProvMns anymore (not sure about that)

NB: given the context just 'message' would have sufficed
ore even better 'reason' as that is the name in the context of the method you are calling next

move PE stuff to 
org.onap.cps.ncmp.impl.data.policyexecutor packagee and/or PolicyExecutor class

probably no need to be ProvMnS specic ie that should not appear in any name

as above
consider moving to setup

no need for able instead use array like so:

It can, I'll add it to a line below.
NB: Could use static imports to make this shorter

NB: Normally test teh happy flow first and then the negative cases, positive scenario va be described shorten then too like so (also change variable names to 'english':
'ready cm handle'
'cmhandle is not ready'  (not the change of phrasing here)
'data producer if is not populated'  (if relevant test both blank and null, i often feel it is good to 'describe' both even if is not really different paths
in that case change the wording to 'null' and 'blank' (test this with one space)
Must be the copyright that makes gerrit think it is a renamed class :-)
remove this part, its clear from the signature (ant not exacrly the request)

be consistent with previous method name
createOPerationDetails

will this not always be delete?! Ie not needed
If you us eteh INtelliJ code check features it will probably point that out too
can this one really be null?!
requires a lot of test, have you covered them all?
'ProvMNS' no longer needed given new context

the name is confusing and simply incorrect I think (it returns null when valid) and it really does 2 things
1) Validation
2) Error response buidling

let's split them!
come to think of it you can use your new ProvMsn InvalidPathException if you make it more generic and store the bits you need for the error response

the more I think about it is does 2 things that belong to different layers
a) Validation -> Should be done in Service Layer (debatable but what we do in most cases)
b) Error response building -> Should be done in REST Layer (obviously!)


Suggestion
rename  and move provms.InvalidPathException to org.onap.cps.ncmp.api.exception.provMnsException (where all the exceptions for all features are)

This validator can do the validation and thrown an exception with the relevant message

in the rest layer catch the exception and build the correct response depending on the type of request, probably in the Controller itself

expand:

GET with data producer null
PUT with data producer blank
PATH with data producer blank

OR (maybe better):

Add the 3 operations (does DELETE use same validatio too?!) to your FIRST (happy) test on line #33

Do your 2 negative scenarios here just with GET
I think I prefer to see this hardcode in the test it is not too big and using triple """ it is easy to do
Its now been moved to RequestValidator
this part belongs in the service layer ie in your validator

dont use words like 'Request' in service layer.
just provmns.Valdiator will do
actually build error Response with dedicated class, just move it back into the REST layer

Each scenario is needed for each endpoint due to if being in controller
